title: Taut foliations and cyclic branched covers
speaker: Cameron Gordon
abstract: Workshop on Flows, Foliations and Contact Structures 2015-2016 Monday, December 7, 2015 - 08:00 to Friday, December 11, 2015 - 12:00 This workshop is part of the topical program "Geometric Structures on 3-Manifolds" which will take place during the 2015-2016 academic year at the Institute for Advanced Study. This workshop will focus on the relations between flows, 2-dimensional foliations, and contact structures on 3-manifolds.
tags: Workshop on Flows Foliations and Contact Structures

proof_stages.label: L-space conjecture framework
proof_stages.summary: The talk sets the standing hypotheses on prime orientable 3-manifolds, recalls left-orderability, coorientable taut foliations, L-spaces, and the theorems linking them, and introduces the temporary dichotomy between total L-spaces and excellent manifolds.

proof_stages.label: Cyclic branched-cover landscape
proof_stages.summary: For cyclic branched covers of prime knots, the speaker reviews motivating known cases: alternating double covers are total L-spaces, torus-knot covers are excellent exactly when their fundamental group is infinite, and two-bridge hyperbolic knots show mixed and largely open behavior.

proof_stages.label: Satellite-cover decomposition
proof_stages.summary: A satellite knot is described from a pattern curve in a solid torus and a companion knot, and its n-fold cyclic branched cover is decomposed along tori into the pattern cover W_n together with d copies of an m-fold cyclic cover of the companion exterior.

proof_stages.label: Boundary-slope gluing for CTF and excellence
proof_stages.summary: The speaker defines CTF, left-orderable, L-space, and excellent multislope sets for torus-boundary manifolds and states a gluing lemma saying that matching CTF or excellent boundary slopes on the pieces yield a CTF or excellent closed manifold.

proof_stages.label: Excellent slopes on knot-exterior covers
proof_stages.summary: Lee--Roberts' theorem supplies intervals of taut-foliation slopes near zero, and after restricting to 1/k slopes and passing to cyclic covers this gives many excellent slopes on the companion-exterior pieces needed for the gluing argument.

proof_stages.label: Cable satellite application
proof_stages.summary: Applying the gluing lemma with the Seifert-fibered pattern-cover piece proves that cyclic branched covers of p,q-cables are excellent except for the double cover of a 2-cable, and the torus-knot companion case of that exception is analyzed via graph-manifold results.

proof_stages.label: Fibered-pattern applications
proof_stages.summary: Using the Lee--Roberts conjectural interval, the large-CTF condition, and Kalelkar--Roberts' theorem on fibered manifolds, the gluing method is applied to untwisted Whitehead doubles and braided satellites to obtain excellence or left-orderability in specified cyclic covers.

proof_stages.label: Satellite conjectures and persistence questions
proof_stages.summary: The talk ends by conjecturing that prime satellite-knot cyclic branched covers should generally be CTF and left-orderable apart from the known 2-cable double-cover exception, and asks whether these properties persist as the branching degree increases.

examples.name: Two-cables of torus knots
examples.note: The double branched covers of 2q-cables of positive torus knots illustrate that the exceptional n=p=2 cable case can split between excellence and total L-space behavior.
examples.engagement_reason: the speaker analyzes the exceptional two-cable case, describes the double cover as two glued knot exteriors, and gives the explicit torus-knot criterion q < 2rs-r-s versus the total-L-space alternative
